micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishnah: Wine vs. Honey, Silver vs. Gold, Rich vs. Poor
The Distinction Between Conditional Stipulations and Object Misrepresentation
Rabbi Shimon's Position: Improvement in the Object Makes Kiddushin Valid

macro_segments.title: Three Interpretations of 'This Goblet': What Is Included in the Gift?

micro_segments.title: Three Baraitot on Whether 'This Goblet' Includes Its Contents
Rashi's Resolution: Water, Wine, and Fish Brine Determine the Meaning

macro_segments.title: Wine and Vinegar: The Storekeeper Analogy and Rabbi Shimon's Disagreement

micro_segments.title: The Gemara's Question: How Does Rabbi Shimon Allow Improvement When Wine/Vinegar Shows Either Party Can Retract?
Tosafot's Distinction: Objects Have Intrinsic Value; Betrothal Focuses on Identity

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Explanation: The Role of a Shaliach and the Concept of Mareh Makom

micro_segments.title: The Woman Appoints a Shaliach to Accept Kiddushin with a Silver Dinar; He Gives Gold
Two Interpretations: Is the Woman Makpid on Silver (Precise) or Using Mareh Makom (Direction)?
The Gemara's Objection: The Mishnah Says 'Marry Me' Not 'Be Married to Him'

macro_segments.title: Rava's Alternative: Woman Instructs Her Shaliach to Accept from 'The Man Who Said Silver'

micro_segments.title: Woman Appoints Shaliach; Man Unexpectedly Gives Gold Instead of Silver Dinar
Is the Woman Just Identifying Which Man ('Mareh Makom') or Requiring Silver ('Makpid')?

macro_segments.title: Three Tannaim Who Hold Mareh Makom: Rabban Shimon, Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel, Rabbi Elazar

micro_segments.title: Abaye Reports: All Three Hold That When You Tell a Shaliach, He Is Just Showing Direction
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel on Get: The Case of Pashut vs. Mkushar and Minhag Hamedina
When Both Pashut and Mkushar Are Acceptable in the Region, What Is the Shaliach Bound By?

macro_segments.title: The Gemara's Conclusion on the Shaliach Cases and the Limits of Makpid/Mareh Makom

micro_segments.title: Mareh Makom Applies Only When the Shaliach Does More Than Asked; Makpid for Less or Switching

macro_segments.title: Three Tannaim Who Hold Mareh Makom: Rabban Shimon, Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel, Rabbi Elazar (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar on Get: Woman Appoints Shaliach at One Place; He Receives at Another

macro_segments.title: Turning to Conditions: Rich vs. Poor—Ulla's Analysis of When Rabbi Shimon Disagrees

micro_segments.title: Ulla: The Debate Is When the Man Turns Out Richer; No Debate on Different Yichus (Status)
Why No One Agrees to Accept Better Yichus: The Woman Cannot Fill His Shoes
Baraita: Rabbi Shimon Admits Even If Yichus Improved, It Is Not Mekudeshet

macro_segments.title: Rav Ashi's Inference from Mishnah Language: Next Mishnah on Kohen/Levi Shows Rabbi Shimon Silent

micro_segments.title: Rav Ashi: The Silence of Rabbi Shimon in the Next Mishnah Proves He Only Disagreed on Wealth
Mar bar Rav Ashi's Objection: The Daughter/Maidservant Case Should Also Show Silence
Gemara's Response: Daughter/Maidservant Falls Under Wealth Category; Only Yichus Is Fundamentally Different
Alternative Answer: Megudelet Means a Woman with Status/Profession (Hairdresser) Creating Discomfort

macro_segments.title: Conditions on Personal Traits: Karaina (Reader), Shoneh (Learner), Talmid (Student), Chacham (Sage)

micro_segments.title: On the Condition I Am a Reader: Three Pesukim Suffice; Three Pesukim with Targum for Precise Reader
On the Condition I Am a Learner: Basic Halachot or Midrash; Or All of Sifra, Sifrei, Tosefta
On the Condition I Am a Student: Ask Him Any One Thing from His Learning and He Answers
On the Condition I Am a Sage: Ask Him About Chochma in Any Field and He Can Analyze

macro_segments.title: Conditions on Physical and Moral Traits: Valiant, Rich, Righteous, Wicked

micro_segments.title: On the Condition I Am Valiant: His Friends Fear Him; Even If He Turns Out Weak, She Is Married
On the Condition I Am Rich: He Is Respected in His Community for His Wealth
On the Condition I Am Righteous: Even If He Is a Complete Wicked Person, Safek Mekudeshet (Shema He Repented)
On the Condition I Am Wicked: Even If He Is Completely Righteous, Safek Mekudeshet (Shema He Fell into Idolatry)

macro_segments.title: Agadic Homily: Ten Measures of Wisdom, Beauty, Wealth, Poverty, Haughtiness, and Other Traits Distributed Among Nations

micro_segments.title: Ten Measures of Wisdom, Beauty, Wealth, Poverty, and Haughtiness: Distribution Among Lands
Haughtiness in Elam: Those Who Learned but Never Merited to Teach Because of Pride
Ten Measures of Strength, Lice, Leprosy, Licentiousness, Brazenness, Chatter, Drunkenness, and Sleep Among Nations
